description_long: This is a medium-sized, ancient-looking ceramic vase with a rounded body and a slightly flared rim. The surface is patchy, showing areas of restoration with lighter, almost white material contrasting with the original reddish-brown clay. Three small, rounded protrusions are spaced around the upper body, possibly serving as decorative or functional lugs. The rim is uneven and appears hand-formed, with visible cracks and signs of age throughout the vessel. The interior is dark and rough, suggesting it has seen significant use or exposure to the elements. The overall appearance is rustic and weathered, indicative of an artifact that has been partially reconstructed.
description: A weathered, partially restored ceramic vase with a rounded body, flared rim, and three small protruding lugs. The surface features contrasting patches of reddish-brown clay and lighter restoration material.
description_view_0: Front view showing the uneven rim, two prominent lugs, and a large patch of lighter restoration material on the lower body.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the rounded form, two lugs, and a more continuous reddish-brown surface with visible cracks.
description_view_2: Angled view displaying the three lugs, the patchwork of restoration, and the vessel's overall curvature.
description_view_3: Rear view with all three lugs visible, a large white restoration patch, and the most pronounced surface wear.
